message:
feat: Add interactive helpers and enhance Traceback renderable

Implement interactive terminal helpers and expand Traceback functionality:

src/interactive.rs (new):
- Status: Spinner + message context helper inspired by Python Rich's
  Console.status() context manager
- Falls back gracefully on non-interactive consoles (prints once)
- Uses Live display for animation when interactive
- Arc<Mutex> message allows updates during operation
- Automatic cleanup on drop

src/renderables/traceback.rs enhancements:
- Add filename field to TracebackFrame for source location
- Add TracebackFrame::filename() builder method
- Add extra_lines field to Traceback for context lines
- Add Traceback::extra_lines() builder method
- Implement source file reading in render() when filename provided
- Display format: "filename:line in function_name"
- Extract and display surrounding source lines when available

src/renderables/json.rs:
- Minor adjustments to JSON rendering

Conformance updates:
- tests/conformance/fixtures/python_rich.json: Add traceback test cases
- generate_fixtures.py: Add traceback fixture generation

The interactive module provides Rust equivalents of Python Rich's
terminal interaction helpers while maintaining the library's focus on
output rendering. The Traceback enhancements enable richer exception
displays with source context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tree nodes may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests to verify and document behavior.

- [ ] Test Tree with styled branches
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output (if markup should work)

## Implementation Notes
- Check Tree implementation for markup handling
- Add explicit tests to lock down current behavior
- May need Tree::add_node_markup() if markup isn't supported

Scope:
- Provide a Pretty renderable for Rust values (Debug) with optional syntax highlighting

- Unit tests cover nested structs/collections
- Document Rust-idiomatic differences vs Python inspect

 {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2nv","title":"Targeted code review pass + file findings","description":"Perform focused code review for correctness/perf risks; file follow-up beads for any findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:53.973055499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601872493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601543252Z","close_reason":"Completed: filed findings bd-yb9 + bd-1wk","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

Ensure adherence to AGENTS.md (manual edits, no deletions).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:36:05.690235412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393505513Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393330824Z","close_reason":"Fix list continuation indent to use cell width for bullet/marker; add regression test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-3c90","title":"Full Python Rich parity (beyond current core)","description":"Owner request: remove/exhaust all parity gaps vs upstream Python Rich (currently 13.9.4 in conformance env).

Track missing modules/features (theme, emoji codes, pretty/inspect, traceback, prompt/input, status/pager, advanced progress, etc), expand conformance fixtures, and update RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY accordingly.

Definition of done:
- All Python Rich public modules/features have Rust equivalents or documented Rust-idiomatic equivalents
- Conformance coverage expanded to cover new features and matches Python outputs where applicable
- README + docs reflect full parity

 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-i1kt","title":"Port rich.emoji: emoji codes + Emoji renderable","description":"Implement Python Rich emoji support.

- Emoji code replacement in strings: :smile:, :smile-emoji:, :smile-text: (with variant selectors) when Console emoji is enabled.
- Provide an Emoji renderable/type similar to Python rich.emoji.Emoji.
- Use the same emoji name->unicode mapping as Python Rich (Rich 13.9.4 rich/_emoji_codes.py).

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for replacement + variants + unknown code passthrough
- Console respects ConsoleBuilder::emoji(false)
- Conformance fixtures include at least one emoji-code case comparing against Python Rich
- Update docs (RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY/README if needed)

             key: Style::new().color_str("blue").unwrap_or_default().bold(),
             string: Style::new().color_str("green").unwrap_or_default(),
-            number: Style::new().color_str("cyan").unwrap_or_default(),

+            number: Style::new()
+                .color_str("cyan")
